title: The lead magnet play for leasing agents
dek: Stop asking strangers to contact me. Start giving them a reason to.
heroAlt: The lead magnet play for leasing agents

blocks.html: Contact me for availability is not an offer. It asks a stranger to start a sales conversation with nothing in return. Almost everyone declines, silently, and the agent concludes their following is worthless. The following was fine. The exchange rate was broken.

A lead magnet fixes the rate: something genuinely useful, delivered instantly, in exchange for an email. A neighborhood price guide. A first-time luxury renter checklist. The ten buildings that allow large dogs. Small, specific, and valuable enough that handing over an email feels like the obvious move.

blocks.text: An email list is the only audience the algorithm cannot take away from you.

items.title: Solve one narrow problem.
items.html: The complete guide to renting converts worse than what $4,000 gets you in three specific neighborhoods. Narrow signals expertise.

items.title: Deliver instantly, then follow up.
items.html: The download arrives in seconds, then a short email sequence introduces who you are and how you work. The magnet opens the door; the follow-up walks through it.

items.title: Mention it everywhere.
items.html: Every post, every bio, every conversation ends with the same pointer. One consistent offer beats five rotating ones.

blocks.html: Draft the title of your first lead magnet today using this formula: [specific outcome] for [specific person] in [your market]. If it would make you click, build it this week.
The free e-book on this site is exactly this play running live. Grab it to see the mechanics from the reader's side.

blocks.lead: This site's free e-book is the play in action.
blocks.label: See the Example
